description: Mobile-first 4×4 letter-trace word game. Drag through adjacent letters to spell words; validates against an embedded dictionary; tracks found words against a target list (all dictionary words reachable on the grid). Fully Lego-composed: imports entities.word-trace-grid + entities.word-dictionary verbatim from the web-components catalog. Touch-only, 100dvh portrait, 44×44 minimum touch targets, pointerdown/move/up handlers (no click events). Carries the canonical mchatai-web-components-used marker for the Lego flywheel.
qualityNotes: Hand-crafted Lego-composed reference for recipe.word-trace. Demonstrates: (1) inline source-copy of WordTraceGrid + WordDictionary (verbatim — passes the Lego provenance gate), (2) mobile-first portrait with 100dvh + safe-area-inset + touch-action:none on the board, (3) pointerdown/pointermove/pointerup with passive:false (mob-005), (4) visible game-over overlay with restart button (mac-022 web equivalent), (5) score/word-count scoreboard, (6) found-words pill tray, (7) prefix-pruning visual cue on currentWord (.is-prefix / .is-valid / .is-invalid), (8) deterministic puzzle list (PUZZLES array — extend with curated boards). Use as the seed when matcher picks recipe.word-trace.

discriminator.reasoning: Hand-crafted by platform team to fill the adjacent-letter-trace word-game genre gap. Composes recipe.word-trace's required Lego components (entities.word-trace-grid + entities.word-dictionary). Mobile-first portrait full-bleed with all mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016) applied. Matcher should route adjacent-letter-trace prompts here; existing word-puzzle-game keeps five-letter-row-guessing prompts via its keywords (five-letter-word-puzzle, six guesses, green yellow gray).
discriminator.overlapNotes: word-puzzle-game is row-of-guesses-shaped (rows × cols of guesses with green/yellow/gray scoring). This template is single-grid-shaped (adjacent-letter trace). Different gameplay; manifests use disjoint matchKeywords + antiKeywords so the matcher routes correctly without ambiguity.
